title: LAM3C
subtitle: 3D sans 3D Scans: Scalable Pre-training from Video-Generated Point Clouds

affiliations.name: University of Technology Nuremberg FunAI Lab

abstract: The bottleneck of 3D self-supervised learning is not only algorithms, but also the scarcity of large-scale 3D data. By turning the vast sea of unlabeled internet videos into point clouds, we unlock a scalable source of 3D supervision. This paper shows that 3D self-supervised learning can be trained using only video-generated point clouds reconstructed from unlabeled videos, without relying on real 3D scans. We introduce RoomTours, a scalable pipeline that converts unlabeled indoor videos into video-generated point clouds, and LAM3C, a 3D self-supervised learning framework designed to learn robust representations from noisy video-generated point clouds. LAM3C transfers well to indoor semantic and instance segmentation tasks across ScanNet, ScanNet200, ScanNet++, and S3DIS benchmarks.
message: The bottleneck of 3D self-supervised learning is not only algorithms, but also the scarcity of large-scale 3D data. By turning the vast sea of unlabeled internet videos into point clouds, we unlock a scalable source of 3D supervision.
tldr: This paper shows that 3D self-supervised learning can be trained using only video-generated point clouds reconstructed from unlabeled videos, without relying on real 3D scans.

contributions.name: RoomTours
contributions.description: A large-scale dataset of 49,219 scenes generated from unlabeled room-walkthrough videos using off-the-shelf reconstruction, enabling scalable 3D learning without traditional sensors.

contributions.name: LAM3C
contributions.description: A noise-regularized self-supervised learning framework with Laplacian smoothing and student-teacher distillation that learns robust 3D representations from noisy video-generated point clouds.

contributions.name: Scaling Results
contributions.description: Superior performance on indoor semantic and instance segmentation tasks across ScanNet, ScanNet200, ScanNet++, and S3DIS benchmarks—all without using any real 3D scans.

results.description: LAM3C with PTv3-Large backbone pretrained on RoomTours49k achieves strong results across multiple indoor 3D understanding benchmarks.

instanceSegResults.description: Instance segmentation results comparing LAM3C with baseline methods across multiple benchmarks.

news.content: Released RoomTours generation code and inference demo visualization.
LAM3C was accepted to CVPR 2026 (main track).
